canonical_name: Transient Receptor Potential Cation Channel Subfamily M Member 4
step_rationale: Ca2+-activated ion channel; proinflammatory signaling in post-MI myocardium and infiltrating leukocytes.
mechanism: TRPM4 activation amplifies proinflammatory Ca2+ signaling in leukocytes and cardiomyocytes, exacerbating plaque inflammation and ischemic injury post-MI.

references.title: Multiomics uncover the proinflammatory role ofdeletion after myocardial infarction in mice.
references.journal: American journal of physiology. Heart and circulatory physiology
